title: Extract integration gate seam
goal: Perform the first behavior-preserving workflow manager seam extraction by moving integration gate and shell command execution responsibilities behind a compact, deep interface without broad mechanical splitting.
acceptance: Extract integration gate and shell command execution into one or more workflow modules with a compact API that returns typed results and does not expose Manager internals or create callback-heavy shallow modules.

Preserve all existing gate and shell-command behavior: cwd/env handling, sh -c execution, output tailing, cancellation checks, timeout handling, process-group cleanup, progress reporting, command result artifacts, and error text semantics.
Manager orchestration remains responsible for lifecycle ordering, retries, repair decisions, checkpointing, and state transitions; the new seam owns command/gate execution details only.
No public CLI contract, daemon IPC contract, status JSON shape, monitor rendering, slice schema, handoff format, or repo config semantics change as part of this refactor.
Tests are added or adjusted where needed so the extracted seam is covered for success, failure, timeout, and cancellation-sensitive behavior without relying only on full daemon happy paths.
docs/workflow-invariants.md is kept accurate after the extraction, especially the integration repair-before-gate and verification timeout invariants.
must_ask_if: The implementation would change public JSON/string contracts rather than preserving them through wrappers or adapters.
The implementation would introduce a new global workflow timeout, change default worker timeout behavior, or make quiet output terminal by default.
The implementation would require broad unrelated splits of workflow::manager beyond the integration gate/shell seam.
